gleam 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference

Compute Milk Production Outputs

Source: R/production_cohort_core_model.R
compute_milk_outputs.Rd

Computes total milk production for a producing cohort (FA) over the assessment period and returns multiple production metrics: total milk mass, milk protein, and fat-protein-corrected milk (FPCM). All outputs are expressed in kg per cohort per assessment period.

compute_milk_outputs(
  cohort,
  milk_yield,
  assessment_duration,
  size,
  milking_fraction,
  milk_protein,
  milk_fat,
  lactose,
  standard_protein,
  standard_fat,
  standard_lactose
)

Arguments

cohort

Character. Sex- and age-specific cohort code describing the production stage of the animals. Supported values include:

  • FA: adult females (from age at first parturition)

  • FS: sub-adult females (from weaning to age at first parturition)

  • FJ: juvenile females (from birth to weaning)

  • MA: adult males (from age at first breeding)

  • MS: sub-adult males (from weaning to age at first breeding)

  • MJ: juvenile males (from birth to weaning)

milk_yield

Numeric. Average milk yield per milk-producing animal during the assessment duration (kg/head/day). This value can be calculated by dividing the total milk destined to human consumption produced per milk-producing animal over the assessment duration by the length of the assessment period.

assessment_duration

Numeric. Length of the assessment period (days).

size

Numeric. Population size in each of the 6 sex–age cohorts: adult females (FA), sub-adult females (FS), juvenile females (FJ), adult males (MA), sub-adult males (MS), and juvenile males (MJ) at the start of the year (heads).

milking_fraction

Numeric. Share of adult females lactating within the assessment duration (fraction). Applies to species: camels (CML), cattle (CTL), buffalo (BFL), sheep (SHP) and goats (GTS).

milk_protein

Numeric. Milk protein fraction (kg protein/kg milk).

milk_fat

Numeric. Milk fat fraction (kg fat/kg milk).

lactose

Numeric. Milk lactose fraction (kg lactose/kg milk).

standard_protein

Numeric. Standard protein content of milk, used to calculate Fat-protein-corrected milk (FPCM), (kg protein/kg milk). Default used=0.033.

standard_fat

Numeric. Standard fat content of milk, used to calculate Fat-protein-corrected milk (FPCM), (kg fat/kg milk). Default used=0.04.

standard_lactose

Numeric. Standard lactose content of milk, used to calculate Fat-protein-corrected milk (FPCM) , (kg lactose/kg milk). Default used=0.048.

Value

Named list containing:

output_milk_mass_production

Numeric. Total milk production produced over the assessment period (kg/herd/assessment period).

output_milk_protein_production

Numeric. Total milk protein production produced over the assessment period (kg protein/herd/assessment period).

output_milk_fpcm_production

Numeric. Total Fat-protein-corrected milk (FPCM) produced over the assessment period (kg/herd/assessment period). Default fat and protein content=0.04 and 0.033.

Non-zero milk outputs are only expected for adult female cohorts. All other cohorts should return zero milk production through upstream parameterisation.

Details

FPCM is calculated using Equation 10 of the International Dairy Federation (IDF) Global Carbon Footprint Standard for the Dairy Sector (IDF, 2022).

References

International Dairy Federation (IDF). 2022. The IDF Global Carbon Footprint Standard for the Dairy Sector. Bulletin of the IDF No. 520/2022. International Dairy Federation (ed.), Brussels, Belgium.

Contents

Developed by Ahmed Jou, Yassine Elaouni, Dominik Wisser, Lydia Lanzoni, Giuseppe Tempio, Federal Ministry of Agriculture, Food and Regional Identity of Germany BMELH.

Site built with pkgdown 2.1.3.
